page: Vultr voc-g-64c-192gb-960s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 64 vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC Genoa processors built on the x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing 3.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes a local 960 GB NVMe SSD for storage. The CPU operates at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz with a single thread per core configuration.

In benchmark testing, this server demonstrates strong processing capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance reaching 3143.25, placing it in the top 10% tier. Its multi-core performance is also robust, scoring 100556.13 in stress-ng tests, which ranks in the top 25%. However, a key architectural tradeoff is the shared CPU allocation model, which may introduce resource contention under sustained, simultaneous multi-tenant workloads despite the high physical core count. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 vector extensions, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16, which accelerate cryptographic and vector-based calculations.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this instance offers a high density of compute cores and memory relative to its resource tier, making it a viable option for parallelizable workloads that do not require dedicated CPU pinning. The combination of 64 cores, 192.0 GB of RAM, and fast NVMe storage makes this server suitable for multi-threaded applications, large-scale web serving, build systems, and intermediate data processing pipelines.
description: Vultr voc-g-64c-192gb-960s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server configured with 64 vCPUs and 192.0 GB of RAM, running on AMD EPYC Genoa processors with x86_64 architecture. The hardware profile includes a 960 GB NVMe SSD and a single thread per core at a 2.0 GHz clock speed. In performance evaluations, the instance delivers top-tier single-core stress-ng results and strong multi-core performance. A notable tradeoff is its shared CPU allocation, which balances high resource density with potential multi-tenant contention. Supported instruction sets include AVX-512 and cryptographic accelerators like AES and SHA_NI. This server is well-suited for parallel processing tasks, multi-threaded applications, and data-intensive web serving workloads that benefit from high core counts and fast local NVMe storage without requiring dedicated CPU resources.
og_description: Vultr voc-g-64c-192gb-960s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server with 64 AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and a 960 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier single-core and strong multi-core performance.
meta_description: Vultr voc-g-64c-192gb-960s-amd features 64 AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and a 960 GB NVMe SSD for high-density parallel processing.
tagline: A high-density AMD Genoa instance featuring sixty-four cores and NVMe storage for parallelized workloads and multi-threaded applications.
bullet_points: 64 AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz
192.0 GB of system memory (3.0 GB per core)
Top-tier single-core and strong multi-core stress-ng benchmark performance
Shared CPU allocation model balancing resource density and cost
Optimized for parallel processing, multi-threaded applications, and web serving
